items.headline: U.S. Futures Attempt Rebound After Thursday's Steep Sell-Off, Oil Prices Ease
items.summary: U.S. stock futures are showing modest gains this morning, signaling a potential recovery after major indices experienced their worst one-day declines since June 23 on Thursday. Investors are assessing mixed Big Tech earnings and concerns over rising capital expenditures for AI. While oil prices have pulled back slightly from Thursday's surge above $100, underlying anxieties about inflation, interest rates, and new tariffs persist. The market sentiment remains cautious, with some analysts describing it as 'frothy' and investors 'walking on eggshells' amidst ongoing geopolitical tensions and a looming Federal Reserve meeting.

sections.title: Major Indices (As of Thursday's Close & Friday's Pre-Market)

items.statContext: Down 1.2% (-90.66 points), worst decline since June 23.
Down 2.2% (-553.21 points), worst decline since June 23.
Outperforming, led by value and traditional sector earnings.

items.headline: Lockheed Martin Rises on Strong Q2 Results
items.summary: Lockheed Martin led gainers in the S&P 500 on Thursday, surging 10.5% after reporting better-than-expected second-quarter results with higher sales and net income.

items.headline: United Rentals Climbs After Q2 Beat
items.summary: United Rentals saw a significant rise of 10.11% following a robust second-quarter earnings beat and an upward revision of its guidance.

items.headline: Thermo Fisher Scientific Up on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific advanced 8.71% after delivering strong second-quarter earnings and raising its full-year guidance.

items.headline: Tesla Plunges on Q2 Disappointment and Spending Concerns
items.summary: Tesla was the biggest S&P 500 loser on Thursday, dropping 14.5% after its second-quarter earnings disappointed and analysts cut price targets due to concerns over heavy investment spending and slowing profitability, particularly in AI-related businesses.

items.headline: T-Mobile US Falls Post-Earnings
items.summary: T-Mobile US declined 10.75% after reporting its second-quarter results, which included softer third-quarter metrics and mixed guidance commentary.

items.headline: Rollins Slides After Weak Q2
items.summary: Rollins stock fell 9.27% following a weaker-than-expected second-quarter performance and subsequent analyst downgrades.

items.headline: Fed Meeting Looms Amid Inflation Worries
items.summary: The Federal Reserve is set to meet next week, with expectations for interest rates to remain unchanged. However, the recent surge in oil prices has prompted investors to increase bets on a potential rate hike later this year, as inflation remains a stubborn issue. The CME Group's FedWatch tool currently indicates a 70.6% probability of the Fed holding rates steady at its July meeting.

items.headline: Oil Prices Fluctuate Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Crude oil prices, which surged above $100 a barrel on Thursday due to escalating Middle East tensions, have eased slightly in Friday's pre-market trading. Brent crude pulled back to approximately $89/barrel. Geopolitical events, including attacks on Saudi oil tankers and threats of military action, continue to fuel fears of prolonged supply disruptions and their impact on global inflation.

items.headline: Key Companies Report Q2 Earnings
items.summary: Verizon Communications ($VZ) reported exceptional second-quarter 2026 results, boosting share buyback targets and raising full-year guidance. Charter Communications ($CHTR) also released Q2 results, with a decline in revenue but an increase in mobile lines. American Express ($AXP), Booz Allen ($BAH), and NextEra Energy ($NEE) are among other major companies scheduled to report earnings today.

items.headline: AI Spending Under Scrutiny Following Tech Earnings
items.summary: Recent earnings reports from major technology companies, including Alphabet and Tesla, have highlighted investor concerns regarding the massive capital expenditures on AI. While Alphabet showed robust demand for AI-related businesses, the focus remained on accelerating spending plans. Tesla's disappointing results added to worries that heavy investment might not translate to immediate profitability, leading to market selectivity.

items.headline: New U.S. Tariffs Take Effect on Global Goods
items.summary: The Trump administration has implemented new tariffs of 10% or 12.5% on goods from 60 trading partners, including China, India, the UK, and the European Union, effective Friday. These duties replace previous temporary tariffs and are aimed at countries failing to prohibit or enforce bans on imports made with forced labor.
